FOREWORD 
 
The Core Banking System (CBS) is the foundation of any bank’s operations, and 
keeping it updated with latest features is essential for delivering efficient customer 
service. Several factors have driven the need to modernize the CBS. These include 
the growing demand for diversified product offerings to meet the evolving customer 
expectations, limitations of legacy systems that hinder innovation and scalability, the 
adoption of new and agile technologies for enhanced operational efficiency, and the 
need  to  remain  compliant  with  evolving  regulatory  requirements  while  mitigating 
technology  risks.  This  modernization  is  crucial  for  ensuring  a  future-ready  Core 
Banking Solution that supports innovation, agility, and operational excellence. 
 
After  the  amalgamation  and  stabilization  of  the  system,  numerous  functionalities 
have been incorporated into the Core Banking System of our Bank. Several process 
flows  have   been   modified   to   align  with   evolving   business   and   operational 
requirements. Data validations, user level restrictions, and various security features 
have  been  integrated  to  meet  functional  and  regulatory  needs. Circulars  and 
Standard Operating  Procedures  (SOPs)  are  issued  for  all  such  enhancements  and 
changes. 
 
The  Bank  has  upgraded  the  existing  front-end  system  from  a  Branch  Server 
environment (.NET platform) to a Centralized Server environment (JAVA platform) 
i.e. New Branch Channel(NBC) which offers stability, scalability and adaptability with 
enhanced security features. 
 
Consolidating all implemented functions in  CBS, a comprehensive  user manual for 
field functionaries have been prepared to carry out their daily operations efficiently 
and effectively. This manual provides step-by-step instructions with examples, key 
features and  troubleshooting  tips  for various functionalities  to  enhance the  overall 
user experience. This will serve as reference for all transactions routed through the 
NBC application. 
 
In  line  with  Bank’s business  requirement, we  are  committed  to  consistently 
enhancing features  in  NBC.  We  expect  this  manual  to  be  highly  beneficial  for 
branches  in  streamlining  their  daily  operations  and  enhancing overall  efficiency. 
Feedback and suggestions from users are always welcome and will be considered for 
incorporation in future updates to the manual. 
 
We acknowledge the efforts of all those involved in the preparation of this manual. 
 
 
 
 
 
Deepak Sarda 
 Chief General Manager – IT & Partnership(CTO/CIO)प्रस्तावना 
 
 
किसी भी बैंि िे परिचालन िी बुकनयाद िोि बैंकििं ग कसस्टम (सीबीएस) होती है; एविं िु शल ग्राहि सेवा 
प्रदान ििने िे कलए इसे नवीनतम सुकवधाओिं से अद्यतन िखना अत्यिंत आवश्यि है। बैंि में सीबीएस िे 
नवीनीििण िी आवश्यिता कवकभन्न िािणोिं से महसूस िी गई है। इनमें, ग्राहिोिं िी बदलती अपेक्षाओिं 
िो पूिा ििने िे कलए कवकवध उत्पादोिं िी पेशिश सिंबिंधी बढ़ती मािंग, नवाचाि एविं प्रगकत में बाधा डालने 
वाली पुिानी प्रणाकलयोिं िी सीकमत सीमाएिं, परिचालन िाययक्षमता िो बेहति बनाने िे कलए नए एविं उन्नत 
प्रौद्योकगकियोिं िो अपनाना तथा प्रौद्योकगिी जोखखमोिं िो िम ििते हुए बदलते कनयामि अपेक्षाओिं िे 
अनुरूप बने िहने िी आवश्यिता शाकमल है। यह नवीनीििण, िोि बैंकििं ग सोल्यूशन िो भकवष्य िे कलए 
तैयाि ििने हेतु बेहद महत्वपूणय है, जो नवाचाि, िु शलता एविं परिचालनगत उत्कृ ष्टता िो सिंबल प्रदान ििता 
है।   
समामेलन एविं कसस्टम िे स्थायीििण िे उपिािंत, हमािे बैंि िे िोि बैंकििं ग कसस्टम में अनेि व्यावहारिि 
कवशेषताएँ शाकमल िी गई हैं। सिंवखधयत व्यवसाय एविं परिचालनगत आवश्यिताओिं िे अनुरूप कवकभन्न 
िाययशील प्रकियाओिं िो सिंशोकधत किया गया है। इसमें व्यावहारिि एविं कनयामि अपेक्षाओिं िो पूिा ििने 
िे कलए डेटा सत्यापन, यूजि लेवल रिखस्टिक्शन एविं कवकभन्न सुिक्षागत कवशेषताओिं िो एिीिृ त किया गया है। 
ऐसे सभी सिंवधयनोिं एविं परिवतयनोिं िे कलए परिपत्र तथा मानि सिंचालन प्रकिया (एसओपी) जािी किए जाते 
हैं।   
बैंि ने मौजूदा फ्रिं ट-एिंड कसस्टम िो ब्ािंच सवयि एनवायिनमेंट (.NET प्लेटफॉमय) से सेंटिल सवयि एनवायिनमेंट 
(JAVA प्लेटफॉमय) अथायत न्यू ब्ािंच चैनल (एनबीसी) में अपग्रेड किया है, जो उन्नत सुिक्षा सुकवधाओिं िे साथ 
खस्थिता, गकतशीलता एविं अनुिू लनशीलता प्रदान ििता है। 
सीबीएस में कियाखित सभी िायों िो समेकित ििते हुए, क्षेत्र पदाकधिारियोिं िे कलए एि व्यापि 
उपयोगिताय मैनुअल तैयाि किया गया है, ताकि वे अपने दैकनि िायों िो िु शलतापूवयि एविं प्रभावी ढिंग से 
सम्पन्न िि सिें । यह मैनुअल, उपयोगिताय िे अनुभव िो बेहति बनाने तथा कवकभन्न िायों िो ििने िे 
कलए; उदाहिण, प्रमुख कवशेषताओिं एविं समस्या कनवािण युखियोिं िे साथ चिण-दि-चिण कनदेश प्रदान 
ििता है। यह एनबीसी एप्लीिे शन िे माध्यम से किए जाने वाले सभी लेनदेन िे कलए सिंदभय पुस्ति िे रूप 
में िाम ििेगा। 
बैंि िी व्यावसाकयि आवश्यिता िे अनुरूप, हम एनबीसी में सुकवधाओिं िो लगाताि सिंवखधयत ििने िे 
कलए प्रकतबध हैं। आशा है कि यह मैनुअल शाखाओिं िो उनिे दैकनि िायों िो सुव्यवखस्थत ििने तथा 
समग्र दक्षता बढ़ाने में अत्यकधि लाभिािी होगा। हम उपयोगितायओिं से प्राप्त फीडबैि एविं सुझावोिं िा 
हाकदयि स्वागत ििते हैं तथा मैनुअल िे भावी अपडेट में उन्हें शाकमल ििने पि अवश्य कवचाि किया जाएगा।    
हम उन सभी िे प्रयासोिं िी सिाहना ििते हैं, कजन्होिंने इस मैनुअल िो तैयाि ििने में अपना योगदान कदया 
है।

- Transaction Threshold Limit (TTL) has been implemented in CASA accounts in order to 
monitor the credit turnover in the account. Under this functionality, monitoring of total 
credit turnover in any Financial Year for an account will be done and system will act 
accordingly in case any credit transaction exceeds the annual threshold  limit of that 
account.  
- A new field named “Expected Annual Credit Amount” has been introduced at account 
level  in CBS  for CASA  accounts where data  as declared by the customer has to be 
captured by the branches during onboarding.  
- In case of existing  account, actual credit  turnover for  the previous Financial Year is 
auto-populated. 
- Branches can capture/change the existing  value under this field upto the  maximum 
ceiling only based on the risk profile. 
- Once queue is passed, system will automatically create the threshold value based on 
the value captured under “Expected Annual Credit Amount” field.  
-  System will allow all the credits in any CASA accounts till it reaches the threshold limit 
of the account. Subsequently, if any specific over the counter credit transactions (i.e. 
Cash  Deposit  /  Transfer  Credits  /  Credit  by  transfer  cheque/  Credit  of  remittance 
instrument) breach the threshold limit of the account, system will ask for supervisory 
override and if accepted by the Branch User, transaction will be through. 
- Passing of above mentioned credit transactions by giving override can be done by the 
branches only thrice (3 times) in a Financial Year.Chapter 2 DEPOSITS                                                                                                                                       Page|  127  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
- After utilization of 3 chances, system will not permit to pass the above mentioned credit 
type transactions unless branch increase the value under “Expected Annual Credit 
Amount” field in order  to enhance  the threshold value of  the  account subject to 
maximum as mentioned in the above table. While capturing value under ““Expected 
Annual Credit Amount” field, branches should ensure that the value should not be lesser 
than the actual credit turnover for the current Financial Year.  
- In case of need based requirement, the value of “Expected Annual Credit Amount” can 
be increased more than the ceiling by ZO users only (User Type 42).  
- In case of any accounts of Govt. bodies/ Quasi Govt. & Other Statutory Bodies and for 
Banks’ Internal Accounts, proper customer type code and product type code has to be 
captured in order to exclude the same from the purview of TTL.

2.5 Opening of Term Deposit – FD/STD/MMD/RD 
Term Deposit is a deposit received by the Bank for a fixed period which is withdrawable only after 
the expiry of  the said  fixed period  and  shall include deposits such as Recurring, Fixed, Money 
multiplier and so on. 
 
(I) Fixed Deposit(FD)  
Fixed Deposit(FD), Short Term Deposit(STD), Money Multiplier Deposit(MMD) are similar schemes 
in which a lump sum received as deposit is repayable at the expiry of a specific period for which 
the deposit is kept. In Fixed  deposit the interest  is payable on simple interest basis while  it  is 
compounded quarterly in money multiplier deposit.

(III) Recurring Deposit Account 
Bank is providing two schemes - Recurring and Variable recurring deposits. 
 
RD: Under the recurring deposit scheme, the account holder deposits a fixed sum of money by 
way  of  monthly  instalments  over  a  stipulated  period  and  on  the  expiry  of  this  period,  the 
instalments paid in are repaid along with interest accumulated thereon, in a lump sum.  
A recurring deposit is accepted with a monthly instalment of Rs.100/- or its multiples. The period 
of deposit should minimum of 6 months and a maximum of 120 months. 
 
VRD: Variable Recurring Deposit scheme has been designed to give the customer the option to 
vary his instalments every month subject to certain conditions, for a fixed period, specified at the 
time of opening the account. 
A Variable recurring deposit is accepted with a minimum instalment of Rs.500/- or its multiples of 
Rs.100/- upto a maximum of Rs.1.00 lakh.  
The customer is given an option to vary his monthly instalments depending  upon  his financial 
position.  The  customer  can  remit  any  amount  in  multiples  of  the  core  deposit  However; the 
maximum amount of deposit per month shall be Rs.10.00 lakh in a calendar month. (No restriction 
on the number of time deposit).  
The customer has to necessarily remit the minimum of core amount as the instalment. The period 
of deposit should be 3 years.

2.6 Renewal of Term Deposit 
All Domestic term deposits viz., Short Term, Fixed and Money Multiplier Deposits are auto rolled 
over for the period restricted to a period of 1 year under the same scheme, at the prevailing rate 
of interest on the date of such automatic renewal applicable to the rolled over amount.  
 If Deposit is placed for 1 year or more, it will be automatically renewed for one year at the 
prevailing rate on Maturity Date.  
 If Deposit is placed for less than 1 year, it will be automatically renewed for same period 
at the prevailing rate on Maturity Date.”  
 
The amount so renewed will be net of TDS amount as applicable, on a case to case basis. The 
process of roll over continues till the Bank receives a demand for payment from the customer. The 
system at the BOD (Beginning of the Day) renews the deposit for the same period of the earlier 
deposit or 1 year (as per the Bank’s policy automatic renewal of Deposits or more than 1 year has 
been restricted to 1 year only) whichever is less. This is termed as ROLLOVER of Deposits.  
If the customer accepts the automatic renewal terms, then the rollover advice for the account can 
be printed and given to the customer to be kept along with the original deposit receipt and there 
is no need to print the receipt again as there is no change in the account number.  
If the same is not accepted by the customer, then following may be performed. 
1. If the customer wishes to close the deposit the same can be done thro the Closure menu 
and the amount may be credited to his Savings/Curent Account.  
2. If the customer wishes to renew the deposit for a period other than that rolled over by 
the system the rolled over deposit may be closed and a fresh account can be opened for 
the  period  desired  by  the  customer.  In  this case,  Term  Deposit  can  be  opened  from 
backdate/rolled over date.Chapter 2 DEPOSITS                                                                                                                                       Page|  136  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
3. Term product can be changed on the customer request on the request date or with effect 
from rollover date.

(b) Term product change on maturity date 
This feature provides customer the flexible renewal options to increase or decrease their deposit 
term value on the maturity without closure of the deposit. The system will process the renewal of 
the TD account on the maturity date, as per the top-up instructions provided by the branch user 
in Screen No.7025. 
Navigation: Deposit → Fixed Term Deposit→ Rollovers/Printing Cheques→ Scr:007025: Rollover 
details 
 Top Up facility will be allowed only for Retail Term Deposits (Less than Rs.3 Crore) 
 KYC Complied Customers are only eligible for top up facility 
 Jointly Operated Term Deposits is not allowed for top up facility. 
 Based on the Customer Request, Branch users have to enter the new term value (greater 
or less than the existing term value), tenor, with new TD category (FD/STD/MMD) through 
a dedicated CBS Screen.  
 If the new term value is greater than the maturity value less TDS, the additional amount 
required for new term value will be debited from the linked SB account of the Customer. 
 If the new term value is less than the maturity amount value, the difference amount will 
be  credited to  the  linked  SB  Account  and  the  account  will  auto  roll  over  with  residual 
balance amount & tenor.Chapter 2 DEPOSITS                                                                                                                                       Page|  139  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
 Home Branch users of the Account can input/modify the top up instructions at least one 
day prior to the maturity date.  
 In the event of insufficient funds in the linked SB on the maturity date for effecting Top 
Up, the system will renew the TD with the maturity value with  less TDS as per Normal 
Term Deposit. 
 RD, Capital Gain Scheme, MACAD, Court Deposits, EMD Deposits, Minor TD Accounts are 
exempted from this facility. 
 If the Loan against Deposit is availed before the date of maturity, top up feature although 
registered will not be executed and the deposit will auto roll over with the original maturity 
less TDS.  
 During the creation of top up instructions, the system will verify product eligibility. If the 
product is ineligible, an error message will be displayed.  
 Eligibility of any new product code entered by the user will be validated. If validation fails, 
an error message will be displayed.  
 Top Up instructions can only be set from the home branch of the TD account. Attempts to 
set instructions from non-home branches will prompt an appropriate error message on the 
Screen (7025). 
 The  system  will  validate  whether  the  new  product  is  active  or  has  been  sunset. If  the 
product has been sunset or the close date is earlier than the maturity date of the account, 
the system will reject the top-up instructions and display an error.  
 If the customer is not maintaining  the balance in SB Account, new term value then roll 
over instructions will not be executed and  account will auto roll over as per the existing 
process 
 If the special product is closed on the date of maturity, account will auto roll over as per 
the existing process or move to ODD Category.  
 The validation restricting top- up instruction to a tenor less than or equal to the existing 
period will no longer apply. Customers may request a longer or shorter tenor during topup 
setup.  
 The system will validate the primary customer's eligibility for the product based on age, 
categorized for senior/super-senior citizens (based on Date of Birth and Interest Category 
characters 2, 5, 8, or 9). If the customer does not meet the age requirement (e.g., under 
60 years for 2 & 5, or under 80 years for 8 & 9), the system will reject the transaction and 
display an error. 
 For staff products, validations for account opening will be performed using the SR number 
check and transactions will proceed accordingly. The system will display an error message 
for customers not meeting the staff criteria.

Back dated opening of Term Deposits is warranted only in following scenarios i.e.  
(i) Customer Requesting for change in tenor in the case of Automated Roll over of existing

(ii) Funds remitted by customer but deposit could not be opened due to delay in receipt of 
funds by branch before EOD, connectivity failure, late clearance 
(iii) Account  wrongly  opened, contrary  instructions  from  customer  in  terms  of  amount, 
product type, product category, periodicity of payment of interest etc.

(II) Duplicate Print Receipt 
When a depositor reports loss of a term deposit receipt, a note should be made in the system. 
Duplicate term deposit receipts should not be issued as a matter of routine. A letter(agreement of 
indemnity) should be obtained from the depositor advising the branch about the loss of the deposit 
receipt and requesting for issue of a duplicate thereof. In case of joint deposit accounts (E or S/A 
or S/other joint accounts), the letter should be signed by all the joint depositors. In case of deposits 
standing  in  the name of an erstwhile minor who has become major at the time of obtaining  a 
duplicate receipt, the letter should be signed by the erstwhile minor only. The signature(s) of the 
depositor(s)  on  the  letter  should  be  verified  by  the  officer  concerned.  When  duplicate  deposit 
receipt is issued, it must be identical with the original receipt in all respects. The date, amount, 
name(s) of depositor(s), rate of interest, period of deposit, due date and  instructions regarding 
operation in joint accounts. A remark reading as under in bold letters is printed at the top on the 
face of the duplicate receipt - 'Duplicate issued in lieu of deposit receipt No..............................dated 
....................lost'.

of duplicate deposit receipt.Chapter 2 DEPOSITS                                                                                                                                       Page|  151

2.13 Opening of Sweep Account 
Sweep accounts are those when balance in the SB a/c or CA goes beyond a certain amount called 
as threshold limit, system transfers the excess amount on a daily basis to a Term Deposit called 
as MOD (Multi Option Deposit) which fetches higher rate of interest. These transactions are called 
as ‘ PUSH’ transactions.  
Whenever a cheque is presented  in  the account and  balance available is insufficient, then  the 
system automatically transfers the required amount from the MOD by closing the latest transferred 
amount in MOD back to the SB a/c or CA and passes the cheque. These transactions are termed 
as ‘PULL’ transactions. 
The total balance in the account can be ascertained by entering the a/c no in an account number 
field and then press F7. The available balance includes the balance in the MOD a/c also.

2.14 Opening of PPF Account 
 Minimum deposit ₹500/- & Maximum deposit ₹1,50,000/- in a Financial year. 
 Loan facility is available from 3rd financial year upto 6th financial year. 
 Withdrawal is permissible every year from 7th financial year. 
 Account matures on completion of fifteen complete financial years from the end of the year 
in which the account was opened. 
 Lock in period of 15 years. After maturity, account can be extended for any number for a 
block of 5 years with further deposits. 
 Account  can  be  retained  indefinitely  without  further  deposit  after  maturity  with  the 
prevailing rate of interest. 
 The amount in the PPF account is not subject to attachment under any order or decree of 
a court of law. 
 Deposit qualifies for deduction under Sec.80-C of I.T.Act. 
 Interest earned in the account is free from Income Tax under Section -10 of I.T.Act. 
 
Eligibility  
1. Individuals in their own name as well as on behalf of a minor or a person of unsound mind 
can open the account. Joint account shall not be opened under this Scheme.  
2. Non-residents/HUF/Association of persons were not eligible to open an account under the 
scheme  
3. The  existing  accounts  opened  for  NRI/HUF/Association  of  Persons  etc.  opened  prior  to 
25/07/2003 are allowed to continue up to the maturity but are not eligible for extension of 
period. The benefits accrued to NRI s will be on non-Repatriation basis 
 
Amount of subscription 
Any individual, on his behalf or on behalf of a minor of whom he is the guardian, can put together 
subscribe under the scheme, minimum of Rs.500/- in multiple of fifty rupees and  maximum for 
Rs.150000/- per year in one lump sum or multiple instalments.  
 
Discontinuation of account 
Any account in which the account holder, having deposited five hundred rupees in the initial year, 
fails to deposit the minimum amount in the following years, shall be treated as discontinued. 
Account may be revived during its maturity period on payment of a fee of fifty rupees along with 
arrears of minimum deposit of five hundred rupees for each year of default: 
The account holder of a discontinued account shall not be eligible to open a new account before 
closure of such discontinued account after maturity 
 
Rate of Interest  
Interest at the rate, notified by the central Government in the official gazette from time to time, 
shall be allowed for calendar month on the lowest balance at credit on an account between the 
close of the fifth day and the end of the month and shall be credited to the account at the end of 
each year irrespective of the change of the account office due to transfer of the account during 
the year.  
 
Period  
Initially the account is opened for a period of 15 years. A subscriber, if he so desires may extend 
the account beyond 15 years for a further block of 5 years 
 
Nomination  
A subscriber may nominate one or more persons to receive the amount standing to his credit in 
the event of his death. Nominations can also be made in respect of an account opened on behalf 
of a HUF. No nomination can however be made in respect of an account opened on behalf of aChapter 2 DEPOSITS                                                                                                                                       Page|  177  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
minor.  The  scheme  provides  for  cancellation/variation  of  nomination which  can  be  made  on  a 
prescribed application form. A subscriber to the fund cannot nominate a Trust as his nominee 
 
Withdrawals from the Fund  
A subscriber may withdraw from the fund, any time after the expiry of five years from the end of 
the year in which the initial subscription was made, from the balance to his credit, an amount not 
exceeding 50% of the amount that stood to his credit at the end of the fourth year immediately 
proceeding the year of withdrawal or at the end of the proceeding year, whichever is lower, less 
the amount of loan. If any, drawn by him, which remains to be repaid. 
 
In the event of the subscriber opting to subscribe for aforesaid block of extension period he shall 
be eligible to make partial withdrawal not exceeding one every year, subject to the condition that 
the total of the withdrawals, during the 5 year block period, shall not exceed 60% of the balance 
at credit, at the commencement of the said period. 
 
Renewal of Account 
PPF account can be revived by paying a fee of Rs.50/- for each year of default along with arrear 
subscription of Rs.500/- for each year of such default with effect from 15.11.2002 
 
Loan facility 
A subscriber can avail a loan, any time after the expiry of one year from the end of the year in 
which the initial subscription was made but before the expiry of 5 years from the end of the year 
in which the initial subscription was made, not exceeding 25% of amount that stood to his credit 
at the end of the second year immediately proceeding the year in which the loan is applied  
A subscriber shall not be entitled to get a fresh loan so long as the earlier loan has not been repaid 
in full together with interest thereon  
The loan can be taken only once in a year even though the loan taken in the year is repaid in the 
same year as the limit of amount of loan is fixed for each year 
Loan repaid within 36 months are subject to an interest rate of 1% per annum from the date of 
loan disbursement. Loan not repaid will attract 6% per annum from the date of loan disbursement.

2.15 Opening of Sukanya Sammriddhi Account(SSA) 
Eligible to open account 
Accounts can be opened only in the name of Girl Child below the age of 10 (on the opening date) 
by natural / legal guardian. 
 
Remittances 
The account may be opened with an initial deposit of Rs.250/- thereafter any amount in multiple 
of Rs.50/- may be deposited subject to minimum of Rs.250/- and maximum of Rs.150000/- per 
financial year.  
Deposits may be made in the account till the completion of a period of fifteen years from the date 
of opening of the account. 
 
Operation of account 
The account will be opened and operated by the natural/legal guardian of the child till the girl child 
in whose name the account has been opened, attains the age of 18 years. On attaining age of 18 
years, the account holder, that is the girl child, may herself operate the account.Chapter 2 DEPOSITS                                                                                                                                       Page|  201  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
Premature closure of account 
In the event of death of the account holder or in case of extreme compassionate grounds such as 
medical support in life-threatening diseases of the account holder or death of the guardian that 
the operation provided that no premature closure of an account under this sub-paragraph shall be 
made before completion of five years from the date of opening of the account. 
 
Withdrawal Facility 
To meet the financial requirements of the account holder for the purpose of higher education and 
marriage, account holder can avail partial withdrawal facility of 50% after attaining 18 years of 
age or after passing Standard 10th, whichever is earlier 
 
Closure on maturity 
The  account  shall  mature  on  completion  of  a  period  of  twenty-one  years  from  the  date  of  its 
opening. 
If the beneficiary is married before maturity of account, account shall be closed.

2.16 Opening of Bulk Deposit 
As per revised  RBI definition of Bulk Deposits, Single Rupee Term Deposits for Rs.3 Crore and 
above is to be classified as Bulk Deposits w.e.f 07.06.2024 and accordingly Retail Term Deposits 
are Single Rupee term deposits of less than Rs.3 crore. 
The interest rate applicable for “Deposit/s of Rs.3 crore and above” and approval for acceptance 
of such deposit/s shall be obtained from Treasury Branch. This condition is also applicable to cases 
where the aggregate value of deposits of an individual customer is of Rs.3 crore and above for the 
day.  Aggregate  value  of  deposits  placed  on  a  day  for  different  tenors/  identical  tenor  will  be 
reckoned for deciding applicable interest rate. Branches are advised to strictly avoid splitting of 
deposits  including  the  rolled  over  deposits  and  quote  the  interest  rate  applicable  for  the  total 
amount for which deposit is required to be placed whether for identical tenor or different tenors. 
1.Only authorized branches are allowed to quote & accept deposits for “Rs. 3 Cr & above after 
obtaining specific approval from Treasury Branch.  
2. During the Day, If Bulk deposits are opened /funded first, then opening of retail deposit (less 
than  Rs.3 Crore)  thereafter  is  restricted  and  also  if  retail  deposit  (less  than  Rs.3 Crore)  is 
opened/funded first, opening of bulk deposits is restricted.  
Illustration: 
If the Customer during the day has opened Retail Deposit of Rs.1.50 Cr, Opening of Bulk Deposit 
of Rs.4 Crore will not be allowed in CBS. 
If the Customer during the day has opened Bulk Deposit of Rs.18 Crore, Opening of Retail Deposit 
of Rs.1.20 Crore will not be allowed in CBS.Chapter 2 DEPOSITS                                                                                                                                       Page|  206  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
Branches to educate the customers on the above restrictions and make all efforts to convince them 
to invest the funds either in Bulk / Retail category during the day. 
3.Total deposit in a single day under single CIF should be below Rs. 3.00 crore to avail Retail Rate 
of interest and system restricts opening of deposits under retail category beyond the threshold 
limit.  
4. Aggregate value of Bulk deposits placed by a depositor on the day for an identical tenor will be 
taken as the basis for deciding applicable interest rate as stated in Para 4.7, Page 21 of Bank’s 
Policy on Deposits / Unclaimed Deposit. 
Based  on  the  feedback  received  from  the  field  functionaries  on  requirement  of  government 
departments for opening of multiple bulk deposits of same tenor whose ROI will be governed by 
the total value of deposit placed during the day.  
Upon approval from the competent authority, a dedicated functionality has been developed in CBS 
where opening of multiple bulk deposits (above 5 Crore) in a single day for the same tenor will be 
allowed at the applicable Card Rate based on the tenor and total value of the deposit against the 
particular CIF.  
Detailed Process Flow for opening of accounts under this functionality: 
 Branches / Zones should mandatorily take the approval from Treasury Branch before quoting 
the rate and opening of single / multiple bulk deposit accounts on receipt of the funds.  
 No Bulk Deposits Accounts of Rs.3 Crore above should be opened without Treasury Approval 
invariably and any deviations from the circularized instructions /SOP will necessitate action 
against the erring official/s.  
 FGMO /  Zones  to  monitor  their  branches  for  ensuring  that  the  circularized  guidelines  on 
opening/ handling of Bulk Deposits are being meticulously followed.

day, 7 are funded and 2 are yet to be opened; In such a case a backdated request has to be 
raised for opening the 2 accounts as per existing backdated account opening process.  Once 
the accounts are opened and funded, a new request is to be raised for the one account that 
was not funded and was closed in the EoD process.   
- If branch fails to open all accounts on same date, the same is to open backdated account as 
per existing process subject to ZO Approval.   
- If all the accounts are opened, but any of them is not funded, in this case branch has to raise 
a new request as due to non-funding of the account, the status of the account will be closed.

2.23 GST Seeding 
Under GST regulations, the bank has to ensure seamless flow of input credit to the Customers 
from whom GST is collected for the banking services, if the customers provide their GST Nos. to 
the Bank.Chapter 2 DEPOSITS                                                                                                                                       Page|  236  
    NBC User Manual V1.0 
To comply with the above GST Regulations and for passing the input credit to the customers in 
respect  of  the  GST  collected  from  them,  the  Bank  has  to  obtain  the  GST  Number/s  of  the 
Customers and update the same at their CIF / Account level.  
Structure of GST No. is given below: 
Total number of digits in GST No  15 
First two digits  Numeral  - State code of the customer 
Next ten digits Number (As per PAN)  PAN No. of the customer 
13th Digit Number  Numeral 
14th Digit Number  Alpha 
15th digit Number  Alpha - Numeral 
 
(I) Updation of GST No in CIF level:  
A facility is provided in CBS for updation of the GST No of the customers who submit / furnish the 
GST No directly at the branch. The branches have to obtain a copy of the GST No. Registration 
Certificate (PID) as proof of allotment of GST No. and keep the same as branch record and update 
the GST No. in the CIF. If customer provides only one GST No for seeding in different accounts 
held by them, the GST No updated at CIF level will be applied  to all the accounts linked to that 
CIF.  
The GST No furnished by the customers will be validated by the System for structural validity as 
above and if they match, the GST No. will be updated in CBS. In case of mismatch in PAN No as 
per CIF and GST No, Branches have to advise the customer to provide the correct PAN with copy 
of the PAN card and verify the same with PAN as per GST No, update the PAN in CIF and then 
seed the GST No in the CIF. In case of mismatch in State Code as per CIF and as per GST No. 
given by the customer, Branches have to advise the customer to provide the address (with proof 
for address) for the GST No provided, for updation of the same at account level.

3.1 Value Paper Inventory Systems(VPIS) 
All the security items used in the Bank are referred to as ‘Value Paper’ in the BANCS system. The 
various numbered/security items can be used by the system only if the details of the items are 
available in the system internally. The system would validate the availability of the respective VPIS 
stock and the serial number with the prefix before it is assigned to an account or a transaction.  
VPIS module in CBS helps us to maintain the inventory of the security items. The stocks of VPIS 
are entered  into the system through Inventory Maintenance menu  available under  VPIS menu. 
Only tellers with user type 40 or 50 can enter the details of the stocks into the system.  
Following are included under VPIS: 
 
S. No. VPIS ITEMS 
1. Current Account Cheques 
2. Savings Bank Cheques 
3. Cash Credit Account Cheques 
4. Traveller Cheques 
5. Bankers cheque (BPO) 
6. Gift Cheques 
7. Fixed Deposit Certificate 
8. Demand Draft (DD) 
9. Welcome kit

The additional details called for in the second screen will depend on the type of inventory entered 
in the first screen. For eg., if stocks of ‘Fixed Deposit Certificate’ is the type of inventory being 
entered into the system through the first screen, the second screen would require only the ‘End 
serial number’ to be entered. 
The entire stock of the security/numbered stationery will be held only in the custody of officer 
designated as VPIS custodian. Though held in single custody, the DD will be available for issue 
by all staff. 
The  stock  of  inventory,  thus  entered  into  the  system,  is  now  available  for  the  Tellers  to  use. 
However, in order to have effective control over the physical custody of the DD/TDRs in 
CBS branches, the stock required for daily use is required to be issued to the officer concerned, 
preferably in multiples of 50/100/500 as the case may be and replenished as and when the stocks 
get exhausted. The details of such physical issue of the security stationery to the officer should be 
recorded in the register maintained for numbered items. The VPIS custodian and the officer 
should initial in the register at the time of issue.

3.2 Cheque Book Functions 
(I) Cheque Book Issue 
In Core Banking, a cheque book can be issued only for the accounts opened with specific product 
codes under Deposit master, viz., SB Cheque account, Current Account, OD/CC account etc., 
In order to issue a cheque book the relevant book must be available in the VPIS of the branch 
under the status ‘Release’. (Refer 3.1) 
Navigation: Cheque Book Functions >> Cheque Book Operations >> Issue Cheque Book Screen 
‘SCR: 050213: CAS: Set/Remove Cheque Book issue’ is displayed.

account later on, by using the same screen and using the option ‘Set’ under Function menu.

(IV) Set/Modify / Remove Stop Payments 
A Stop payment instruction in an account is recorded at the request of the customer and should 
be carried out only on getting a written request. The cheque number(s) to be stopped must have 
been issued against the account.
